# Hamma

> Clean-room Rust mesh networking stack targeting Tailscale compatibility.

Hamma starts with `dictyon`, a peer client that validates against tailscale.com before the fleet builds `histos`, the sovereign coordination server. Phase A is pre-alpha and currently points at the WireGuard data-plane milestone.

## Binary

`dictyon` - planned/active peer-client surface under `crates/dictyon`.

## Architecture

Structured LLM corpus:
- [_llm/architecture.toml](_llm/architecture.toml): workspace shape, planned crates, and layers
- [_llm/current_state.toml](_llm/current_state.toml): phase and open threads
- [_llm/decisions.toml](_llm/decisions.toml): accepted mesh-stack decisions
- [_llm/glossary.toml](_llm/glossary.toml): hamma vocabulary

## Agent Entry Points

- [README.md](README.md): public overview and phase plan
- [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md): repo conventions and build/test rules
- [Cargo.toml](Cargo.toml): current workspace members
- [standards/](standards/): kanon-synced standards
